programs.programName: Nebraska SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
Home-Delivered Meals (via Area Agencies on Aging)

verified.notes: Age 60 is the consistent senior-specific threshold across all Nebraska Area Agencies on Aging home-delivered meals programs in search results. Phone number is for Eastern Nebraska Office on Aging (ENOA) Meals on Wheels program serving Cass, Dodge, Douglas, Sarpy, and Washington counties. Search results do not contain specific income or asset limits for the home-delivered meals program; eligibility criteria emphasize functional need (homebound, difficulty preparing meals, lack of nutritional support) rather than financial thresholds. Low-income individuals may receive assistance through Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services, but specific income/asset caps are not documented in provided sources.

programs.programName: Nebraska Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)

verified.notes: Age requirement (55+) confirmed from Nebraska Department of Labor official source. Income limit exists (125% of federal poverty level per multiple sources) but specific dollar amounts are not provided in search results and vary by household size/composition. No asset limits found in search results. Official phone number for Nebraska SCSEP application/enrollment not located in provided search results; contact information should be obtained directly from https://dol.nebraska.gov/ReemploymentServices/Training/SCSEPProgram or local American Job Centers.

verified.notes: Age 60+ is the statewide senior threshold for Legal Assistance via Nebraska Area Agencies on Aging and Legal Aid of Nebraska. Asset limits ($12,000 individual / $24,000 couple) are documented for Lancaster County (Aging Partners) but appear to be applied statewide; no conflicting asset limits found in search results. Income limits not specified in retrieved sources. ElderAccessLine (800-527-7249) is the primary statewide intake number. Program is active as of February 2026 per Aging Partners documentation.
